tossups.question: As a young man, this artist painted a self-portrait imagining himself at age 100 in which he sits on a divan and is menaced by a creature he especially hated, a spider. This artist incorporated oriental vases and dolls from a curio shop owned by his mother Marie into many of his paintings. This artist’s best known painting was kept rolled up for thirty years and is now at the Getty Museum. This artist represented himself as the title fish in a piece mocking his unfriendly critics titled (*) Skeletons Fighting Over a Pickled Herring. This artist wears a red feathered hat in a crowded canvas depicting himself surrounded by people wearing masks. A procession of mask wearers largely ignore the title figure underneath a banner reading “Vive La Sociale” in another of this artist’s works. For 10 points, name this Belgian artist of Christ's Entry Into Brussels in 1889.
tossups.answer_sanitized: James Ensor [or James Sidney Edouard, Baron Ensor]
tossups.question_sanitized: As a young man, this artist painted a self-portrait imagining himself at age 100 in which he sits on a divan and is menaced by a creature he especially hated, a spider. This artist incorporated oriental vases and dolls from a curio shop owned by his mother Marie into many of his paintings. This artist's best known painting was kept rolled up for thirty years and is now at the Getty Museum. This artist represented himself as the title fish in a piece mocking his unfriendly critics titled (*) Skeletons Fighting Over a Pickled Herring. This artist wears a red feathered hat in a crowded canvas depicting himself surrounded by people wearing masks. A procession of mask wearers largely ignore the title figure underneath a banner reading "Vive La Sociale" in another of this artist's works. For 10 points, name this Belgian artist of Christ's Entry Into Brussels in 1889.

bonuses.leadin: A colorful Ernst Kirchner painting in the Art Institute of Chicago depicts the “Apparition of these Entities” to Eulenspiegel. For 10 points each:
parts: One of these entities is shown riding another and wearing a Hitler mask in an allegorical work by Otto Dix. These entities are depicted in a wheel alongside the “Four Last Things” in a painting by Bosch.

This American artist included a spiny, red lion man in a series of grotesque depictions of the seven deadly sins. This artist depicted drunken sailors frolicking with women in his 1934 painting The Fleet’s In.

This Belgian painter included The Seven Deadly Sins Dominated by Death in a print series, though he may be better known for including skeletons under a banner reading “Vive la Sociale” in Christ’s Entry into Brussels.
answers: the seven deadly sins [accept the capital vices; accept the cardinal sins]
answers_sanitized: the seven deadly sins [accept the capital vices; accept the cardinal sins]
James Ensor [accept James Sidney Edouard, Baron Ensor]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: A colorful Ernst Kirchner painting in the Art Institute of Chicago depicts the "Apparition of these Entities" to Eulenspiegel. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: One of these entities is shown riding another and wearing a Hitler mask in an allegorical work by Otto Dix. These entities are depicted in a wheel alongside the "Four Last Things" in a painting by Bosch.

This American artist included a spiny, red lion man in a series of grotesque depictions of the seven deadly sins. This artist depicted drunken sailors frolicking with women in his 1934 painting The Fleet's In.

This Belgian painter included The Seven Deadly Sins Dominated by Death in a print series, though he may be better known for including skeletons under a banner reading "Vive la Sociale" in Christ's Entry into Brussels.

bonuses.leadin: A dead, anthropomorphic, plucked chicken in the process of laying an egg hangs on the wall in a work by this man that shows his own head on a platter with a smoked herring. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this Belgian painter who depicted Octave Maus and Edmond Picard as the title figures in The Dangerous Cooks. He is best known for his monumental Christ’s Entry Into Brussels.
The rejection of Ensor’s The Oyster Eaters by the L’Essor group was one of the causes for the formation of this other group of Belgian artists, named for the number of its primary members.

Though he was not an original member, this admirer of Seurat eventually joined Les XX. His take on pointillist technique can be seen in his Beach at Heist, which shows a yellow and pink sunset behind an oddly shaped cloud.
answers_sanitized: James Ensor [or James Sidney Edouard, Baron Ensor]
Les XX [or Les Vingt; accept The Twenty]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: A dead, anthropomorphic, plucked chicken in the process of laying an egg hangs on the wall in a work by this man that shows his own head on a platter with a smoked herring. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this Belgian painter who depicted Octave Maus and Edmond Picard as the title figures in The Dangerous Cooks. He is best known for his monumental Christ's Entry Into Brussels.

Though he was not an original member, this admirer of Seurat eventually joined Les XX. His take on pointillist technique can be seen in his Beach at Heist, which shows a yellow and pink sunset behind an oddly shaped cloud.

bonuses.leadin: Answer the following about the history of skulls in painting, for 10 points each.
parts: This painting by Hans Holbein the Younger depicts a diagonally-distorted skull at the feet of the two title professionals.
This subgenre of Dutch still-life paintings often depicted skulls on tables as a symbol of the emptiness of life.

This artist and member of Les Vingt depicted two skulls playing tug-of-war over a pickled herring with their teeth in one of his skeleton paintings. He also created a large crowd scene in which Jesus visits his nation's capital, in which a large red banner reads "VIVE LE SOCIALE."
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Answer the following about the history of skulls in painting, for 10 points each.
parts_sanitized: This painting by Hans Holbein the Younger depicts a diagonally-distorted skull at the feet of the two title professionals.
This subgenre of Dutch still-life paintings often depicted skulls on tables as a symbol of the emptiness of life.

This artist and member of Les Vingt depicted two skulls playing tug-of-war over a pickled herring with their teeth in one of his skeleton paintings. He also created a large crowd scene in which Jesus visits his nation's capital, in which a large red banner reads "VIVE LE SOCIALE."

bonuses.leadin: A fuzzy black hat adorns the figure on the right side of this man’s painting Skeletons Fighting over a Pickled Herring. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this artist who painted a more famous work in which a roundel depicting the crucifixion hangs above figures with bloody hands. At the bottom left of that painting, frogs jump out of a black cauldron.
Besides painting The Vile Vivisectors, Ensor created this enormous work in which a banner proclaiming “Vive La Sociale” frames the entrance of a religious figure on a donkey.
Christ’s Entry into Brussels hangs in this city’s J. Paul Getty Museum. Frank Gehry designed this city’s Walt Disney Concert Hall.
